title: 78 Years Since the Nakba: Ongoing Displacement, Denied Return, and Enduring Resistance
content:
Today we mark the Nakba, the ‘catastrophe’, and remember the more than 750,000 Palestinians forcibly expelled from their homeland in 1948, some 530 villages destroyed and the families forced to live in an exile that has never ended. For many families, their only ties to home and hope of return are now their house keys, which they hold on to.

Seventy-eight years later, almost six million Palestinians remain registered as refugees. The Nakba was never a single date in history. It is a continuing reality, sustained through occupation, siege and the denial of return.
